mnemonics.title: Inner Product Properties
mnemonics.content: SCIP: Symmetric (conjugate), Conjugate symmetry; Linearity in first argument; Inner product positive-definite; Parallelogram law: ||u+v||² + ||u-v||² = 2(||u||² + ||v||²)

mnemonics.title: Gram-Schmidt Steps
mnemonics.content: Take first vector as is; For each next vector: subtract its projection onto each previous orthogonal vector to get orthogonal component, then normalize if needed

mnemonics.title: Operator Types
mnemonics.content: NUN: Normal - NTT* = T*T; Unitary - Preserves norm (U*U = I); Hermitian - Equal to adjoint (H* = H)

mnemonics.title: Cauchy-Schwarz Usage
mnemonics.content: Remember: always product of norms, absolute value of inner product ≤ product of norms

acronyms.usage: |<u,v>| ≤ ||u|| ||v||, fundamental bound in inner product spaces
Converts linearly independent set to orthogonal/orthonormal set

visualTips.visualization: Using cos θ = <u,v>/(||u|| ||v||), the angle is acute if dot product positive, obtuse if negative, right if zero
Draw vector v, draw subspace W as a line/plane, drop perpendicular from tip of v to W - that's the projection
Each new vector has its components along previous orthogonal directions subtracted, leaving only perpendicular component
Hermitian operators: stretch along perpendicular axes, eigenvalues show stretching amounts, all real

analogies.concept: Inner product as dot product generalization
analogies.analogy: Like extending distance from 1D number line to 2D plane to 3D space - still measures angle and length but in any dimension
Like perpendicular streets - they never intersect, they cross at 90° angle, no overlap in 'direction'
Like taking a group of friends who all point in random directions and rotating each so they all point at right angles to each other
Like the transpose mirror - reflects what the original operator does but in the 'dual' space, flipping rows and columns
